2603.15994.title: Selective Memory for Artificial Intelligence: Write-Time Gating with Hierarchical Archiving
2603.15994.paper_summary: Retrieval-augmented generation stores all content indiscriminately, degrading accuracy as noise accumulates. Parametric approaches compress knowledge into weights, precluding selective updates. Neither mirrors biological memory, which gates encoding based on salience and archives rather than deletes superseded information. We introduce write-time gating that filters incoming knowledge objects using composite salience scores (source reputation, novelty, reliability) while maintaining version chains that preserve prior states. Using real LLM evaluation without oracle access to quality labels, write gating achieves 100 percent accuracy versus 13 percent for ungated stores. The critical finding emerges under distractor scaling: at 8:1 distractor ratios, read-time filtering (Self-RAG) collapses to 0 percent while write gating maintains 100 percent, revealing a structural advantage of write-time over read-time curation. Validation on Wikipedia (20 entities), procedurally generated pharmacology data, and 2026 arXiv papers confirms these findings. The gating advantage scales inversely with parametric memory support: +25pp for Wikipedia, +48pp for post-cutoff arXiv, +65pp for procedural data with zero training knowledge. Signal ablation confirms the method does not depend on oracle-correlated metadata. Write gating matches Self-RAG accuracy at one-ninth the query-time cost.
2603.15994.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強生成無差別地儲存所有內容，隨著噪聲的累積而降低準確性。參數化方法將知識壓縮為權重，排除了選擇性更新的可能性。這兩者都無法反映生物記憶的特性，生物記憶根據重要性來控制編碼，並存檔而非刪除被取代的信息。我們引入了寫入時閘控，使用綜合重要性分數（來源聲譽、新穎性、可靠性）過濾進來的知識對象，同時保持保留先前狀態的版本鏈。使用真實的LLM評估而不依賴於質量標籤的預測，寫入閘控的準確率達到100％，而無閘控儲存的準確率僅為13％。關鍵發現出現在干擾物縮放下：在8:1的干擾物比例下，讀取時過濾（Self-RAG）的準確率崩潰至0％，而寫入閘控則保持100％，顯示出寫入時策劃相對於讀取時的結構優勢。對維基百科（20個實體）、程序生成的藥理數據以及2026年的arXiv論文進行的驗證確認了這些發現。閘控優勢與參數記憶支持呈反比：維基百科提高25個百分點，截止後的arXiv提高48個百分點，程序數據在零訓練知識的情況下提高65個百分點。信號消融實驗確認該方法不依賴於與預測相關的元數據。寫入閘控的準確率在查詢時間成本的九分之一時與Self-RAG相匹配。
2603.15994.author: Oliver Zahn et.al.
